findings.description: Off-by-one: guard uses `<=` so a client with exactly `limit` recorded hits is still allowed through, appending a (limit+1)-th timestamp and enforcing a ceiling of limit+1 instead of limit. Fix: change to `< self.limit`.

`remaining` is computed after `hits.append(now)`, so it underreports by 1 and goes negative (-1) when the off-by-one on line 43 permits the extra request; should be computed before the append or use `self.limit - len(hits)` pre-append.

`self._hits[key]` on a `defaultdict` materialises and permanently inserts an empty bucket for every unseen key, including on read-paths; an adversary cycling arbitrary X-Forwarded-For values causes unbounded dict growth with no cap or eviction until sweep() runs.

Client identity is the first element of the caller-supplied X-Forwarded-For header with no validation; any client can spoof an arbitrary IP to impersonate another tenant or rotate forged IPs to bypass the rate limit entirely.
`list.pop(0)` is O(n); under a full window of hits every `check()` call degrades to O(limit) — use `collections.deque` with `popleft()` for O(1). Deferring detail to critic-performance.

`current_usage()` neither prunes stale entries nor shares a `now` timestamp with `check()`, so its count can diverge from what `check()` would enforce; callers relying on it for pre-flight decisions will see a stale or inconsistent view.

No thread-safety: `_hits` is a plain `defaultdict` with no lock; concurrent requests from the same client can both pass the `len(hits) < limit` check before either appends, allowing simultaneous over-limit requests in any multi-threaded WSGI/ASGI deployment.

`retry_after = hits[0] + self.window - now` can yield a negative value at floating-point boundary conditions (hit exactly at cutoff not pruned due to strict `<`), which would produce an invalid negative Retry-After for the caller.

`sweep()` removes a bucket only when all timestamps are outside the window but does not compact partially-stale buckets, so large hit lists for quiet clients remain in memory until every timestamp ages out rather than being pruned progressively.
